title: psoralea aurea
intro: Psoralea aurea, also known as the Indian turnip or golden crown, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the Indian subcontinent. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate the thyroid gland and promote the metabolism of iodine. The health benefits of Psoralea aurea include its potential to improve thyroid function, boost energy levels, and enhance immune system function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of capsules, tablets, or extracts, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential interactions with thyroid medications.
